meta.title: Algebra Foundations: Pre-Algebra & Algebra 1, Live Online
meta.description: Live online Pre-Algebra and Algebra 1: repair fractions and negatives, then master linear equations, functions and quadratics. 8 one-hour classes a month.
meta.level: Grades 7-9, or any student whose algebra base needs a real rebuild
meta.commitment: 2 live one-hour classes per week + short practice sets
meta.certification: Course completion certificate + phase mastery checks

program_overview.title: Algebra Foundations Masterclass
program_overview.tagline: Algebra is a language. We teach your child to speak it, not memorize it.
program_overview.description: A 12-month live program covering Pre-Algebra and the whole of Algebra 1, the single most consequential stretch in school mathematics. Every later course (Geometry, Algebra 2, SAT, calculus) silently assumes this material, and most struggling high-schoolers trace their trouble to exactly here. We teach algebra as a language with meaning: negatives and fractions rebuilt first, letters introduced as numbers-in-waiting, equations read as sentences, and functions met as machines. Students solve and explain aloud in every class; procedures are drilled only after the idea underneath holds.
what_makes_this_different: The arithmetic base, fractions, negatives, order of operations, is repaired first, because algebra built on sand always collapses in year two
Letters taught as meaning, not ritual: students can say what x is doing in every line they write
Word problems as the main course, not the scary extras at the chapter's end
Equation-table-graph fluency: every linear situation seen three ways
The explain-back method: what survives explaining is what is actually learned
One dedicated mentor who tracks exactly where understanding thins
A genuine on-ramp to Algebra 2, SAT prep and beyond, this course is the runway
One full interactive hour per class, twice a week

learning_path.phase_1: Pre-Algebra: the number system mastered, integers, fractions, decimals, ratios and the first variables
learning_path.phase_2: The heart of Algebra 1: equations, inequalities, linear functions and systems, seen as equation, table and graph
learning_path.phase_3: Exponents, polynomials and quadratics, plus the word-problem and graph craft that makes it all usable

career_outcomes: An algebra base that makes Algebra 2, Geometry and SAT prep feel fair
Word-problem confidence, the skill every later exam actually tests
Grade improvement that persists, because it rests on understanding
Readiness for advanced tracks: honors math, competition math, and math-through-coding

phase_1_foundation.title: PHASE 1: Pre-Algebra, The Number System Mastered (Weeks 1-16)
phase_1_foundation.description: Almost every algebra struggle is an arithmetic gap wearing a disguise. This phase rebuilds integers, fractions, decimals and ratios to genuine fluency, then introduces variables so gently they feel obvious.

week_1_4.title: Weeks 1-4: Diagnostic + Integers and the Number Line
topics: Full diagnostic: where the number base actually stands
The number line as home: integers, opposites and absolute value
Adding and subtracting negatives with meaning (temperature, money, elevation)
Multiplying and dividing negatives, why the rules are what they are
Order of operations as grammar, not a mnemonic chant
Mental arithmetic habits started: every class opens with fluency work
projects: Personal gap map for the year ahead
Integer war-stories set: temperature, bank balance and elevation problems
week_1_4.practice: Do a short daily integer practice set this week, using the number line whenever unsure, and write one real money or temperature story for a negative number calculation.

week_5_10.title: Weeks 5-10: Fractions and Decimals, Properly This Time
week_5_10.theme: The gap behind most algebra failures
topics: Fractions as numbers with places on the line, not pizza slices
Equivalence and comparison through reasoning, not rules
All four operations with fractions, each from a picture first
Decimals, place value and fraction-decimal-percent conversion fluency
Estimation as a checking habit: does this answer make sense?
Exponents introduced as repeated multiplication with meaning
projects: Fraction operation gallery: one worked picture per operation, made by the student
Real-recipe scaling project: fractions doing real work
week_5_10.practice: Complete a mixed fractions and decimals problem set, drawing a quick picture for any operation you cannot yet explain in words.

week_11_16.title: Weeks 11-16: Ratios, Percents and the First Variables
week_11_16.theme: From arithmetic to algebra, without the cliff
topics: Ratio and rate reasoning: the choose-the-easy-comparison skill
Proportions solved by sense first, cross-multiplication second
Percent problems in every costume: change, discount, tax, reverse
Variables introduced as numbers-in-waiting; expressions as phrases
Evaluating and simplifying expressions; like terms with meaning
The distributive property as area, a picture that never lies
projects: Best-buy investigation: unit-price reasoning on real products
Phase mastery check: solve, explain and extend, per topic
week_11_16.practice: Solve ten percent problems taken from real life, discounts, tax and score changes, estimating each answer before computing to check your number sense.

phase_2_linear_algebra.title: PHASE 2: Equations, Functions and Systems (Weeks 17-36)
phase_2_linear_algebra.description: The heart of Algebra 1. Equations are read as sentences and solved with balance reasoning; linear functions are met as equation, table and graph telling one story; systems make two stories intersect. This is the phase that decides the next five years of math.

week_17_22.title: Weeks 17-22: Equations and Inequalities as Sentences
topics: One-step and two-step equations from balance reasoning
Multi-step equations: variables on both sides, distribution, fractions
Translating words into equations, the skill that unlocks word problems
Literal equations: isolating any variable in a formula
Inequalities: what flips the sign and why
Checking solutions as a habit, not an afterthought
projects: Equation-sentence dictionary: twenty phrasings, twenty equations
Real-world setups: phone plans, savings goals, break-even problems
week_17_22.practice: Work a set of multi step equations showing the balance move at every line, then translate three word problems into equations without solving them.

week_23_29.title: Weeks 23-29: Linear Functions Three Ways
topics: Coordinate plane fluency and graph-reading discipline
Slope as rate of change, from stories before formulas
Slope-intercept, point-slope and standard form, translated freely
Graphing lines by meaning; intercepts as starting values
Function notation and the function-as-machine idea
Linear modeling: real data fitted, interpreted and questioned
projects: Equation-table-graph triptych for three real situations
Data modeling mini-project: fit a line, defend the interpretation
week_23_29.practice: Graph the same line from slope intercept, point slope and standard form to confirm they agree, and describe one real situation the line could model.

week_30_36.title: Weeks 30-36: Systems of Equations and Inequalities
topics: Systems by graphing: the intersection as the answer to a question
Substitution and elimination, chosen by structure not habit
How many solutions? Parallel, crossing and identical lines as ideas
Systems word problems: mixtures, comparisons, break-even
Linear inequalities and systems of them, with shaded-region reasoning
Phase checkpoint: mixed problem set at grade-level exam standard
projects: Business break-even case solved all three ways
Phase mastery check: solve, explain and extend, per topic
week_30_36.practice: Solve six systems choosing the method by structure, at least two each by graphing, substitution and elimination, and check every answer in both equations.

phase_3_quadratics.title: PHASE 3: Exponents, Polynomials and Quadratics (Weeks 37-52)
phase_3_quadratics.description: The bridge into Algebra 2: exponent rules from meaning, polynomial arithmetic without fear, factoring as detective work and quadratics met as functions with real stories. The course closes with the word-problem and graph craft that turns knowledge into marks.

week_37_42.title: Weeks 37-42: Exponents and Polynomials
topics: Exponent rules derived, not memorized, including zero and negatives
Scientific notation with real scale (atoms to galaxies)
Polynomials as expressions with structure: naming, adding, subtracting
Multiplying polynomials: the area model that makes FOIL obvious
Special products recognized as patterns: squares and difference of squares
Fluency drills at pace, now that meaning holds
projects: Powers-of-ten scale tour: notation doing real scientific work
week_37_42.practice: Complete an exponent rules drill, deriving any rule you forget instead of guessing, plus five polynomial multiplications using the area model.

week_43_48.title: Weeks 43-48: Factoring and Quadratic Equations
topics: Factoring as un-multiplying: GCF, trinomials, special patterns
The zero-product property, why factoring solves equations
Square roots and solving by square-rooting
The quadratic formula, derived once so it is never mysterious
Choosing a method by structure: factor, root, or formula
The discriminant previewed: how many solutions and why
projects: Factoring pattern-book, built by the student
Quadratic equation gauntlet: mixed methods under light timing
week_43_48.practice: Factor a 15 expression mixed set, then solve five quadratic equations choosing factoring, square roots or the formula and noting why each time.

week_49_52.title: Weeks 49-52: Quadratic Functions + the Capstone
topics: Quadratic functions and parabola anatomy: vertex, axis, intercepts
Projectile and area stories: the vertex as the answer to a real question
Graphing quadratics from every form
Course capstone review: the year's ideas connected into one map
Algebra 2 preview: what comes next and why this base carries it
Final mastery check across all three phases
projects: Capstone: one real quadratic situation modeled, graphed and presented
Personal algebra handbook, the year's ideas in the student's own words
week_49_52.practice: Graph two parabolas from different forms, labeling vertex, axis and intercepts, and start your capstone map connecting the year's big ideas.

faqs.question: Who is this course for?
faqs.answer: Three groups: students in grades 7-9 meeting algebra for the first time and wanting it taught properly; older students whose Algebra 2 or SAT struggles trace back to a weak Algebra 1 base; and homeschool families who want a structured, live-taught algebra year. The diagnostic in the free demo places every student accurately.

faqs.question: My child is already failing algebra at school. Is this remedial or advanced?
faqs.answer: It is neither and both, it is understanding-first. We start where your child actually is (the diagnostic finds it), repair the arithmetic underneath if needed, and build to full Algebra 1 ownership. Struggling students stop sinking; comfortable students go deeper than school does.

faqs.question: Why does the course spend weeks on fractions and negatives?
faqs.answer: Because they are the gap behind most algebra failures. A student who cannot operate on fractions confidently will lose points in every algebra topic forever. We fix the foundation first, it is slower in week one and dramatically faster by month six.

faqs.question: How is this different from school algebra?
faqs.answer: Class sizes and method. School must march thirty students through a syllabus; we teach 1-on-1 or in tight small groups, with your child solving and explaining aloud for a full hour. Every idea is built from meaning, balance reasoning, area models, function machines, so it survives past the test.

faqs.question: Will this align with my child's school curriculum?
faqs.answer: Yes. The course covers the standard Pre-Algebra and Algebra 1 territory (US Common Core, and the equivalent stretch in Indian, British and IB middle-school systems), and the mentor sequences topics to reinforce what school is doing rather than clash with it.

faqs.question: How long until we see grade improvement?
faqs.answer: Homework and test comfort usually move within 4-6 weeks; deep, durable improvement, the kind that persists into Algebra 2, builds over the year. Monthly billing means you watch evidence and re-decide every four weeks.

faqs.question: What does the course cost?
faqs.answer: Small-group classes are ₹1,499 per month and private 1-on-1 mentorship is ₹4,999 per month in India; international students pay USD $100 per month for group and $150 for 1-on-1. Both include 8 live one-hour classes a month, recordings, and a free demo class before any payment.

faqs.question: Can we try it before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every student starts with a free live demo class that doubles as the diagnostic, a real lesson plus an honest read on where the gaps actually are, with no card details required.

testimonials.name: Shewta Singh, mother of Ishan (verified Google review)
testimonials.text: My son struggled with math for years. Integrating it into coding projects has transformed his understanding and confidence. Highly recommended!

testimonials.name: Sonam Oswal, mother of Dhairya (verified Google review)
testimonials.text: My child Dhairya is really enjoying the classes. This is his first online class, and he eagerly looks forward to it. I can see his improvement.

Fraction operation gallery and factoring pattern-book, made by the student
Business break-even case solved three ways
Capstone quadratic modeling project + personal algebra handbook
skills_mastered: Linear functions as equation, table and graph

prerequisites.education: Typically grades 7-9; older students rebuilding a weak base are equally welcome

prerequisites.equipment: Computer with stable internet, notebook and pencil
prerequisites.time_commitment: 2 live hours plus 1-2 practice hours weekly
prerequisites.motivation: Willingness to explain thinking aloud, the method depends on it

who_is_this_for.first_timers: Grade 7-9 students meeting algebra for the first time who deserve to meet it properly
who_is_this_for.strugglers: Students drowning in school algebra whose real gap is usually fractions or negatives
who_is_this_for.rebuilders: Older students whose Algebra 2, SAT or IGCSE trouble traces back to this exact material
who_is_this_for.homeschoolers: Families who want a structured, live-taught algebra year with a real teacher
who_is_this_for.advancers: Strong grade 6-7 students ready to start the algebra road early

career_paths_after_completion: Ready for Algebra 2, Geometry and the high-school mathematics track
The base that SAT and ACT math prep assumes
Eligible for competition mathematics and honors placements
Continue with the High School Mathematics Mastery Program with the same mentor

course_guarantees.live_classes: Live, interactive classes with a real instructor, never pre-recorded videos.
course_guarantees.small_batches: Small batches only: group classes are capped at 10 students, with mini-batch (3 to 4 students) and personal 1-on-1 options.
course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
course_guarantees.free_demo: A free demo class before you enrol, so you can decide with no pressure.
